fact: June 2 is exactly 153 days into the year - that's the same number as how many fish the disciples caught in the Bible!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when fireflies start their crazy blinking dance parties in backyards and forests.
The sun rises before 5:30 AM in most northern cities on June 2, which means birds start their morning concerts while you're still dreaming!
June 2 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season - the zodiac sign of the twins, which explains why the weather can't decide if it wants to be hot or cool.
In Alaska, June 2 brings nearly 19 hours of daylight - imagine trying to convince your parents it's bedtime when the sun is still shining!
This is peak strawberry season in many places, when the berries are so sweet and juicy they practically burst in your mouth.
June 2 is only 19 days before the summer solstice, making it one of the year's most energetic days as Earth tilts toward maximum sunshine.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, June 2 marks the heart of autumn when leaves turn brilliant orange and red.
Cicadas emerge from underground around this time in their 13 or 17-year cycles, creating a buzzing symphony so loud it can drown out lawnmowers.
The moon on June 2 can appear extra bright and colorful when it's low on the horizon, thanks to all the thick summer air it has to shine through.
June 2 is when many flowers release their strongest perfumes to attract bees and butterflies - nature's own aromatherapy session!
In northern regions, this date brings the phenomenon of 'white nights' where twilight lasts for hours and true darkness never really arrives.
